skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guides users through designing, scaffolding, validating, and packaging AI skills for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.summary: Create structured AI skills with clear workflows, useful resources, validation checks, and package-ready output.
skill.author: AstraBit-CPT

security_audit.summary: Static network, weak-cryptography, and shell-execution matches are mostly false positives from Apache license text, Markdown fences, and template strings. The real risk is local filesystem automation: the initializer writes into user-provided paths without enforcing its documented naming rule, and the packager archives every file under a selected skill directory. No evidence found for network calls, credential exfiltration, malicious intent, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Skill Directory Path
medium_findings.description: scripts/init_skill.py accepts a skill name and output path, then creates directories and writes SKILL.md plus resource files. It documents a hyphen-case naming rule but does not enforce it before combining user input into the destination path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence that command-line inputs flow into filesystem creation and writes without name validation. Risk is limited because the script only runs when a user or agent explicitly invokes it.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Files
medium_findings.description: scripts/package_skill.py recursively archives every file under the selected skill directory. It has no denylist for hidden files, private notes, or secret-like filenames, so users can unintentionally package local sensitive files stored inside a skill folder.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit and lacks filtering. This is a legitimate packager design, but it creates exposure risk if users place sensitive files in the skill directory.

low_findings.title: License URLs Are Not Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs appear only in the Apache license text. They are not used by code and do not indicate outbound network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URL matches are static license references, not executable code. No network library or request call was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misread As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution matches are Markdown fences, inline examples, or strings written into generated templates. No subprocess, os.system, shell backtick, or dynamic command execution pattern was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation or template text. The Python scripts do not invoke shell execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Weak-Cryptography Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts correspond to prose, Markdown, YAML metadata, and validation text. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or password storage logic was present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain license language, validation rules, and writing examples rather than cryptographic operations. No evidence found for weak cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Filesystem Write Target
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer combines command-line input with a destination path and writes several files. A stricter skill-name validator would reduce accidental or crafted path expansion.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path construction and file writes are directly visible. The concern is scoped to local script execution, not remote compromise.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unfiltered Recursive Archive
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager includes every regular file under the skill directory without filtering sensitive or hidden filenames.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The archive loop is clear and broad. The risk depends on user directory contents.

content.value_statement: Building reusable AI skills is hard when workflows, resources, and packaging rules are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through skill design, scaffolding, validation, and packaging.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure SKILL.md files with clear frontmatter and concise body instructions.
Guides users through planning scripts, references, and assets for reusable skill workflows.
Provides a local initializer script that creates a starter skill directory with example resources.
Provides a validator for basic frontmatter, naming, and description checks.
Provides a packager that validates a skill and creates a distributable .skill archive.
Includes reference guidance for workflow patterns and output pattern design.
limitations: The initializer does not enforce the documented skill-name rule before writing files.
The packager archives all files inside the selected skill directory without secret filtering.
The validator performs basic structural checks, not deep security review or quality scoring.
Users must customize or delete generated placeholder resources before publishing.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Define the skill purpose, generate a starter structure, and replace placeholders with team-specific workflows.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Packaging
use_cases.description: Validate a finished skill folder and package it into a shareable .skill archive.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Skill Instructions
use_cases.description: Refactor a large SKILL.md into concise core guidance with separate references for advanced details.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a new skill for [task]. Ask the most important questions first, then propose the SKILL.md sections and useful resources.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Starter Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a starter skill named [skill-name] in [directory]. Use the initializer, then summarize which placeholder files I should keep, edit, or remove.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Skill Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing skill for clarity and progressive disclosure. Suggest which content belongs in SKILL.md and which content belongs in references or scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate and package this skill folder for distribution. Report validation issues, inspect the package contents, and warn me about files that should not be included.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for creating release notes from merged pull requests.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with trigger description, workflow sections, a suggested release-note template reference, and guidance on whether a helper script is useful.
A validation result, package creation status, and a short review of included files that may need cleanup before sharing.

output_examples.input: My skill file is too long and hard to follow.
output_examples.output: A restructuring recommendation that keeps core workflow steps in SKILL.md and moves detailed examples into targeted reference files.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused on essential workflow guidance and move detailed variants into referenced files.
Validate skill names and inspect output directories before running scaffolding or packaging scripts.
Review package contents before sharing a .skill archive with other users.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated placeholder files without replacing or deleting them.
Do not store secrets, private notes, or unrelated files inside a skill directory.
Do not overload the skill body with long examples that should be separate references.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create structured AI skills with SKILL.md guidance, optional resources, validation, and packaging.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it validate skill security?
faq.answer: No. The included validator checks basic structure, names, and descriptions. It does not perform a deep security audit.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found for network calls. The only URLs reviewed are Apache license references.

faq.question: What should users check before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should inspect the skill folder and remove secrets, private files, generated placeholders, and unrelated content.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The bundled scripts write files and recursively package directories. These are useful features, but they need careful path and content review.
